published_prior_art.cite: gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds and rosenman_rajkumar_gauriot_slonim_2019_discrete_manipulation do not give a shared many-neighborhood retained-mass image
rosenman_rajkumar_gauriot_slonim_2019_discrete_manipulation gives discrete manipulated-RD bounds, not this laminar TU/state-flow oracle
chernozhukov_lee_rosen_2013_intersection_bounds supplies generic intersection-bound inference, not this RD-specific dual/witness

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Bertanha 2020 is a many-threshold/multiple-cutoff RD paper about counterfactual ATEs, while §6 fixes one cutoff c and varies nested neighborhoods; the comparator differs along the cutoff-vs-window axis.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The rdmulti consumer comparison is overstated: Cattaneo-Titiunik-Vazquez-Bare 2020 targets multiple cutoffs or scores, not a manipulation-robust joint frontier over nested windows around one cutoff.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The headline names dual potentials (alpha_j, eta_jw, nu_j), but Exhibit 9.2 does not compute the dual variables or verify dual feasibility/complementarity on the J=1 instance.

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: Computation rubric is only partially delivered: graph-size bounds are stated on an assumed prefix-compressed face, but the advertised §6 fixed-lambda oracle still needs a corrected residual term and a non-promissory dual exhibit to clear the flagship floor.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The score slack sigma_j=lambda_j M_j-sum I_j n_b y_b omits the left-limit outcome term M_j sum_w pi_{j,-}(w) mu^Y_{j,-}(w), so theta_j(n)<=lambda_j is not equivalent to sigma_j>=0 unless zero left means are added.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the score state with a centered score increment that includes the left-limit outcome residual, or add a zero-left-mean normalization assumption to every theorem using STATEFLOW.
Compute the actual J=1 dual potentials in Exhibit 9.2 and check dual feasibility plus complementary slackness against the parent endpoint program.

Either reformulate the setup as true multiple cutoffs to justify Bertanha/rdmulti, or replace those comparisons with bandwidth/window-choice and manipulation-robust RD references closer to nested neighborhoods around one cutoff.

To reach flagship, derive prefix compression from primitive assumptions or extend the oracle beyond the assumed prefix-compressed face to the unrestricted finite-support many-window problem with an explicit complexity guarantee.

recommended_next_step: Revise the computation kernel: fix the residualized score algebra, make the dual exhibit non-promissory, and realign the published comparator chain with nested-window RD rather than many-cutoff RD.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal adds explicit graph-size and J=1 boundary computations, but the fixed-lambda oracle is algebraically incomplete and the dual recovery object is not yet exhibited.
